topThreeIssues: Staircase order lists 'show recursion tree' as rung 5 (after defining both conditions) but the lesson correctly builds the tree before defining conditions -- the staircase description should be reordered to match the actual pedagogical sequence to avoid confusing lesson planners.

The erase-and-replace transition at 175-187s leaves the F(5) root and level-1 nodes (F(4), F(3)) on the canvas while drawing the new linear chain in the same 'center' region -- specify that all tree nodes are erased or relocated to avoid visual clutter when the linear chain is introduced.

The memoization code block uses a global mutable array and sys.setrecursionlimit, which are Python implementation workarounds rather than DP concepts; replace with a dict-based or functools.lru_cache version to keep the code focused on the memoization pattern.

Board canvas clutter at the GQA transition: the erase actions at t=120 remove only the head diagrams but leave kvcache_box, mha_heads_label, title_mha, mqa_label, and bottleneck_label on the canvas when GQA content begins -- add erase actions for all MHA/MQA ancillary elements or make the region strategy explicit so a renderer knows what survives into the GQA section.

Payoff field mislabels G: it says 'reduction by a factor of G (number of query heads divided by number of KV heads)' but G is the number of KV groups, not the reduction factor (which is H/G). This introduces a terminology inconsistency right at the hook sentence -- rewrite as 'GQA cuts KV-cache memory by a factor of H/G, where H is the number of query heads and G is the number of KV-head groups.'

Beat at t=48 is overloaded with two conceptually distinct ideas -- the quantitative cache formula and the qualitative bottleneck framing (memory bandwidth vs. FLOPs) -- which are both important enough to deserve separate beats; splitting them would improve pacing and allow the board to write the formula before the bottleneck label, matching the narration order more cleanly.

The narration introduces the decomposition formula (beat at t=20) before Bias and Variance are defined (beats at t=32 and t=55), creating a forward-reference that could confuse intro learners; the staircase should define terms before presenting the formula they compose

The pacing gap between t=160 and t=240 has no narration beat despite 80 seconds remaining, and the fullText narration ends at approximately 200 seconds worth of content, suggesting the durationSeconds is over-allocated or a concluding beat summarizing the key takeaway is missing
The term 'data-generating process' in the sigma^2 paragraph is used without definition, which is a jargon gap for intro-level learners who may not know what a DGP is

The narration introduces 'variational inference', 'VAE', and 'MLE' at the t=204 beat without any unpacking -- at intermediate level these are high-leverage application anchors and even a one-clause gloss ('MLE, the standard way of fitting models to data') would sharpen clarity without adding length.

The t=90 and t=204 beats each carry two staircase rungs simultaneously; splitting the forward-KL zero-avoidance explanation (currently covering both the infinity argument and the mass-covering consequence) into two beats with a board action between them would reduce cognitive load at the densest moment.

The highlight board actions at t=30 and t=62 specify only the targetId as content rather than which portion of the formula to highlight (e.g., the log-ratio term, or the outer weighting factor), leaving a renderer without enough information to produce a precise highlight -- replace content with a specific sub-expression or a region description.

Bradley-Terry loss is introduced as named jargon without being unpacked -- define it as a pairwise comparison loss or explain why it produces the stated formula to close the only significant narration-clarity gap.

The KL formula on the board specifies 'in bits' in narration but does not mark the log base, creating a minor but real inconsistency; either annotate log_2 in the formula or remove the 'in bits' qualifier from narration.

The upper-left region (pretrained_obj, pi_ref_label, gap_label, gap_arrow) is never explicitly erased when the three-stage column layout is built at t=33-36, risking canvas clutter; add erase actions for those elements at t=32-33.

Math/notation consistency: the t=60 board note 'divide by p' (inverted dropout training-time scaling) appears before the lesson has introduced inverted dropout, conflating standard and inverted dropout conventions mid-lesson and potentially confusing learners about when each scaling applies.

Code clarity: nn.Dropout(p=1-p) uses mismatched variable semantics without sufficient inline annotation -- the outer constructor's p means keep-probability while PyTorch's Dropout p means drop-probability; an intro learner will likely misread the API usage.

Destination-narration mismatch: the lesson.destination references 'geometric mean of all 2^n subnetwork predictions' but the narration never uses the term 'geometric mean', leaving a conceptual gap between what the lesson promises and what it delivers.

No draw actions anywhere in the board plan -- the topic has strong geometric content (data cloud, projection arrow, variance spread) that is never visualized, leaving learners with pure text on a visually rich concept

The orthogonality of successive principal components is implied in narration ('orthogonal to the first') but never derived or placed in the staircase, leaving an unexplained claim for intermediate learners

Beat 118-145 covers constrained optimization (Lagrangian setup through gradient-zero derivation) in 27 seconds without unpacking the gradient step, which may lose learners who have not seen Lagrange multipliers before

Math inconsistency in the spectrum example: the spectrum_sketch draw action at t=110 draws bars of height 2 for x(t)=2sin(...), but the write action at t=128 labels |F(440)|=1 -- fix by making both the diagram and the text agree on the correct amplitude (1 for each delta when using the bilateral convention with the factor-of-2 split, or 2 if not splitting).

The 'complete' in 'complete orthogonal family' and the 'arg' function used in narration are left undefined for an intermediate learner -- add a one-sentence gloss for each ('complete means every signal in L-squared can be approximated arbitrarily well'; 'arg is the angle of the complex number in the plane').

The linearity rung in the staircase never appears in narration or board actions -- either remove it from the staircase or add a beat and board action that explicitly demonstrates linearity (e.g., F(x+y)=F(x)+F(y)), since listing it in the staircase without teaching it misleads a reader about the lesson's scope.

Board visual balance is the biggest gap: only one draw action exists and it appears near the end; EM's core geometric insight -- the ELBO as a lower bound surface touching the likelihood curve -- demands at least one diagram earlier (e.g., at ~t=65 when the ELBO is introduced), showing log p(X|theta) as a curve above a touching parabola labeled ELBO.

The code block at t=130 lands inside the already-dense M-step beat window, adding a third distinct idea; moving it to a dedicated beat after t=155 (post-ascent-proof) would clarify that the GMM code illustrates the full E+M cycle rather than just the M-step.

The 'bounded above by zero' convergence claim is presented without qualification; for continuous models p(X|theta) can exceed 1, so the argument should invoke monotone convergence for bounded-above sequences or restrict the claim to discrete Z, avoiding a factual imprecision for the advanced learner audience.

Board visual balance is severely underdeveloped -- only one draw action exists for a topic with strong diagrammatic affordances; adding a two-branch probability tree or a belief-update bar would materially improve comprehension.

The derivation step (staircase rung 4: 'derive Bayes' rule from conditional probability') is promised but never executed -- the formula is simply stated; either deliver the derivation or remove that rung.
The final ~60 seconds of board actions (t=194-240) have no corresponding narration beats, leaving the audio-visual sync undefined for the lesson's closing visual.
